servinfo.title: Camshaft and Tappet Removal and Installation

s1.ptxt: Remove right engine undercover.
Remove cylinder head cover.
Turn crankshaft clockwise and adjust No.1 cylinder to TDC as follows.

s2.ptxt: Align both VVT actuator and exhaust camshaft timing sprocket marks (1) with surface of cylinder head (2) respectively.
Align notch (1) on crankshaft pulley with “0” (2) indication on timing chain cover.

s1.ptxt: Remove timing chain cover plug (1) and gasket (2).
Fix timing chain tensioner (1) as follows.

attention2.ptxt: Use bolt as specified below for fixing timing chain tensioner.

s2.ptxt: Tighten timing chain tensioner fixing bolt (2) by hand to fix timing chain tensioner (1).
While turning crankshaft 20° counterclockwise, tighten timing chain tensioner fixing bolt (2) by hand again.
While turning crankshaft 20° clockwise this time, tighten timing chain tensioner fixing bolt (2) by hand again.

s1.ptxt: Apply a dab of paint to two timing chain links (2) which meet timing marks (1) on VVT actuator and exhaust camshaft timing sprocket.
Remove exhaust camshaft timing sprocket as follows.

s2.ptxt: Hold the hexagonal section of exhaust camshaft using a wrench.
Remove exhaust camshaft timing sprocket bolt, then remove sprocket (1).

attention2.ptxt: Do not turn camshaft after removing exhaust camshaft timing sprocket.

s1.ptxt: Loosen camshaft housing bolts in numerical order (“1” – “21”) evenly and gradually.
Remove intake camshaft and exhaust camshaft.
Remove camshaft bearings and tappets, if necessary.
Remove VVT actuator from intake camshaft as follows, if necessary.

s2.ptxt: Hold hexagonal section of intake camshaft using vice.
Remove VVT actuator bolt and remove VVT actuator (1).

s1.ptxt: Install VVT actuator (1) to intake camshaft as follows, if removed.

s2.ptxt: Hold hexagonal section of intake camshaft using vice.
Install VVT actuator and tighten VVT actuator bolt to specified torque.

s1.ptxt: Apply engine oil to contact surfaces of each tappet (hatched area), and install tappets (1) to cylinder head, if removed.
Install camshaft bearings as follows, if removed.

s2.ptxt: Fit tab (1) of camshaft bearing to groove (2) of cylinder head or camshaft housing.
Press camshaft bearing end (3) until the bearing is firmly seated on the cylinder head or camshaft housing.
Apply engine oil to sliding surface (4) of camshaft bearings.

attention2.ptxt: Do not apply engine oil to mating surface (5) between bearing and camshaft housing or cylinder head.

s1.ptxt: Apply engine oil to sliding surfaces of camshafts.
Install camshafts to cylinder head as follows.

s2.ptxt: Align the painted timing chain link (2) with timing mark (1) on VVT actuator and then install intake camshaft.
Install the exhaust camshaft so that the dowel pin (3) is positioned upwards.

s1.ptxt: Install camshaft housings (1) to proper places distinguished by letter (2), number (3) and arrow direction (4) on each camshaft housing.

entry.ptxt: Point arrowhead to timing chain side

s1.ptxt: Tighten camshaft housing bolts as follows.

s2.ptxt: Install camshaft housing No.1 (1).
Install camshaft housing bolts and tighten them by hand.
Tighten camshaft housing bolts in numerical order (“1” – “21”) evenly and gradually.

s1.ptxt: Install exhaust camshaft timing sprocket (3) to exhaust camshaft as follows.

s2.ptxt: Install special tool to exhaust camshaft.
Align the painted timing chain link (1) with timing mark (2) on exhaust camshaft timing sprocket (3).
Install exhaust camshaft timing sprocket (3) with timing chain to the exhaust camshaft.

s1.ptxt: Tighten exhaust camshaft timing sprocket bolt (1) using special tool.

attention2.ptxt: When using an extension special tool with a torque wrench, the reading value of the torque wrench is smaller than specified torque. When using extension special tool, reading value should be calculated using the formula below.

spec.title: Formula for reading value of torque wrench.

item.ptxt: M: Reading value using extension special tool

s1.ptxt: Confirm that painted timing chain links (2) are aligned with timing marks (1) on VVT actuator and exhaust camshaft timing sprocket.
Remove timing chain tensioner fixing bolt from timing chain cover.
Install timing chain cover plug (1) with new gasket (2), and tighten it to specified torque.
Check that camshaft and timing chain are installed properly as follows.

s2.ptxt: Check that VVT actuator and exhaust camshaft timing sprocket marks (1) are aligned with top face of cylinder head (2).
Check that crankshaft pulley mark (1) is aligned with “0” (2) of timing chain cover.
Turn crankshaft clockwise fully twice and check again that match marks are aligned.

item.ptxt: Be sure to turn crankshaft 720°. If it is turned only 360°, the timing marks (1) on VVT actuator and exhaust camshaft timing sprocket will not meet top face of cylinder head (2) as shown in figure.
After turning crankshaft 720°, the painted links of the timing chain will not be aligned with the timing marks on the VVT actuator and exhaust camshaft timing sprocket, but this is normal.

s1.ptxt: Check valve clearance.
Install cylinder head cover.
Run the engine and check for engine oil leaks.
Install right engine undercover.
